sections.label: Section 456
sections.original: τί βλέφαρα τέγγεις δάκρυσι ; πρὸς τίνʼ οἰκτρὸς εἶ ;
sections.translation: Why are your eyes wet with tears ? To whom are you lamenting ?

sections.label: Section 457
sections.original: πρὸς τὰς πάροιθεν συμφορὰς εὐδαίμονας .
sections.translation: To my fortunes , which were happy before this .

sections.label: Section 458
sections.original: οὔκουν ἀπελθὼν δάκρυα σοῖς δώσεις φίλοις ;
sections.translation: Well then , why don ’ t you go away and give these tears to your friends .

sections.label: Section 459
sections.original: τίς δʼ ἥδε χώρα ; τοῦ δὲ βασίλειοι δόμοι ;
sections.translation: What is this land ? Whose palace is this ?

sections.label: Section 460
sections.original: Πρωτεὺς τάδʼ οἰκεῖ δώματʼ , Αἴγυπτος δὲ γῆ .
sections.translation: Proteus lives here , the land is Egypt .

sections.label: Section 461
sections.original: Αἴγυπτος ; ὦ δύστηνος , οἷ πέπλευκʼ ἄρα .
sections.translation: Egypt ? O wretched , that I have sailed here !

sections.label: Section 462
sections.original: τί δὴ τὸ Νείλου μεμπτόν ἐστί σοι γάνος ;
sections.translation: And why do you blame the bright gleam of the Nile ?

sections.label: Section 463
sections.original: οὐ τοῦτʼ ἐμέμφθην · τὰς ἐμὰς στένω τύχας .
sections.translation: I do not blame it ; I am sighing for my fate .

sections.label: Section 464
sections.original: πολλοὶ κακῶς πράσσουσιν , οὐ σὺ δὴ μόνος .
sections.translation: Many people are doing badly ; you are not the only one .

sections.label: Section 465
sections.original: ἔστʼ οὖν ἐν οἴκοις ὅντινʼ ὀνομάζεις ἄναξ ;
sections.translation: Is the king you name in the house ?

sections.label: Section 466
sections.original: τόδʼ ἐστὶν αὐτοῦ μνῆμα , παῖς δʼ ἄρχει χθονός .
sections.translation: This is his tomb ; his son rules the land .

sections.label: Section 467
sections.original: ποῦ δῆτʼ ἂν εἴη ; πότερον ἐκτὸς ἢ ʼν δόμοις ;
sections.translation: And where might he be ? Abroad , or in the house ?

sections.label: Section 468
sections.original: οὐκ ἔνδον · Ἕλλησιν δὲ πολεμιώτατος .
sections.translation: He is not inside ; he is most bitterly opposed to the Hellenes .

sections.label: Section 469
sections.original: τίνʼ αἰτίαν σχὼν ἧς ἐπηυρόμην ἐγώ ;
sections.translation: What cause does he have ? I have felt the consequences of it !

sections.label: Section 470
sections.original: Ἑλένη κατʼ οἴκους ἐστὶ τούσδʼ ἡ τοῦ Διός .
sections.translation: Helen , the daughter of Zeus , is in this house .

sections.label: Section 471
sections.original: πῶς φῄς ; τίνʼ εἶπας μῦθον ; αὖθίς μοι φράσον .
sections.translation: What do you mean ? What did you say ? Tell me again .

sections.label: Section 472
sections.original: ἡ Τυνδαρὶς παῖς , ἣ κατὰ Σπάρτην ποτʼ ἦν .
sections.translation: The daughter of Tyndareus , who once lived in Sparta .

sections.label: Section 473
sections.original: πόθεν μολοῦσα ; τίνα τὸ πρᾶγμʼ ἔχει λόγον ;
sections.translation: Where did she come from ? What is the meaning of this ?

sections.label: Section 474
sections.original: Λακεδαίμονος γῆς δεῦρο νοστήσασʼ ἄπο .
sections.translation: She came here from the land of Lakedaimon .

sections.label: Section 475
sections.original: πότε ; οὔ τί που λελῄσμεθʼ ἐξ ἄντρων λέχος ;
sections.translation: When ? Surely I have not been robbed of my wife from the cave ?

sections.label: Section 476
sections.original: πρὶν τοὺς Ἀχαιούς , ὦ ξένʼ , ἐς Τροίαν μολεῖν .
sections.translation: Before the Achaeans went to Troy , stranger . But get away from the house ; for something is happening within , by which the palace is thrown into confusion . You have not come at the right time ; and if my master

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: λάβῃ σε , θάνατος ξένιά σοι γενήσεται .
sections.translation: catches you , death will be your guest-gift . For I am well-disposed to Hellenes , for all that I spoke harshly to you in fear of my master .

sections.label: Section 483
sections.original: τί φῶ ; τί λέξω ; συμφορὰς γὰρ ἀθλίας
sections.translation: What can I say ? For after my former troubles , this present event that I hear of is an unhappy one ,

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original: εἰ τὴν μὲν αἱρεθεῖσαν ἐκ Τροίας ἄγων
sections.translation: if I have come here , bringing my wife who was taken from Troy , and she is kept safe in the cave , but some other woman who has the same name as my wife lives in this house . She said the woman was born the child of Zeus .

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἦ τις ἔστι Ζηνὸς ὄνομʼ ἔχων ἀνὴρ
sections.translation: Can there be a man with the name of Zeus by the banks of Nile ? For there is only one in heaven . Where in the world is there a Sparta , except by the streams of Eurotas , with its lovely reeds ? The name of Tyndareus is the name of one alone .

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: Λακεδαίμονος δὲ γαῖα τίς ξυνώνυμος
sections.translation: Is there any land of the same name as Lakedaimon or Troy ? I do not know what to say ; for there are probably many things in the wide world that have the same names , both cities and women ; there is nothing , then , to marvel at .

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: οὐδʼ αὖ τὸ δεινὸν προσπόλου φευξούμεθα ·
sections.translation: Besides , I will not run away from a servant ’ s fears ; for no man is so barbaric at heart as to refuse me food when he has heard my name . The fire of Troy is famous , and I , Menelaos , who lighted it , am well known in every land .

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: δόμων ἄνακτα προσμενῶ · δισσὰς δέ μοι
sections.translation: I will wait for the master of the house ; he gives me two things to look out for : if he is a cruel sort of person , I will keep myself hidden and go back to the shipwreck ; but if he shows any softening , I will ask for help in my present state .

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: κακῶν μὲν ἡμῖν ἔσχατον τοῖς ἀθλίοις ,
sections.translation: This is the worst evil for me in my misery , to beg the means of life from other kings , when I am myself a king ; but it is necessary . The saying is not mine , but it is a wise word : nothing is stronger than dreadful necessity .

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: ἤκουσα τᾶς θεσπιῳδοῦ κόρας ,
sections.translation: I have heard the prophetic maiden , who gave a clear answer within the palace : Menelaos is not yet dead and buried , gone to the land of shadows where darkness takes the place of light ;

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἔτι κατʼ οἶδμʼ ἅλιον
sections.translation: but he is still wearing out his life on the ocean swell and has not yet reached the haven of his country , wretched in his wandering life ,

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: παντοδαπᾶς ἐπὶ γᾶς πόδα
sections.translation: bereft of every friend , approaching every land in his sea-going ship from the land of Troy .

sections.label: Section 528
sections.original: ἥδʼ αὖ τάφου τοῦδʼ εἰς ἕδρας ἐγὼ πάλιν
sections.translation: Here I am , once again coming back to the sanctuary of this tomb , after learning the welcome words of Theonoe ,

sections.label: Section 530
sections.original: ἣ πάντʼ ἀληθῶς οἶδε · φησὶ δʼ ἐν φάει
sections.translation: who knows all things truly ; she says my husband is alive and sees the light of day ; he is roaming here and there on countless voyages , not without practice in wandering , and he shall come here when he finds an end to his suffering .

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original: ἓν δʼ οὐκ ἔλεξεν , εἰ μολὼν σωθήσεται .
sections.translation: But she left one thing unsaid : if he will escape when he has come ? And I refrained from asking that question clearly ; I was so glad when she told me he was safe . She said that he was near this land somewhere , cast up , shipwrecked , with a few friends .

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: ὤμοι , πόθʼ ἥξεις ; ὡς ποθεινὸς ἂν μόλοις .
sections.translation: Oh , when will you come ? How much I long for your arrival !

sections.label: Section 541
sections.original: ἔα , τίς οὗτος ; οὔ τί που κρυπτεύομαι
sections.translation: Ah ! Who is this ? I am not being ambushed by the plots of Proteus ’ impious son , am I ? Shall I not , like a young racehorse or a worshipper of Bacchus , reach the tomb ? There is something wild

sections.label: Section 545
sections.original: μορφὴν ὅδʼ ἐστίν , ὅς με θηρᾶται λαβεῖν .
sections.translation: about the looks of this man who is hunting me down .

sections.label: Section 546
sections.original: σὲ τὴν ὄρεγμα δεινὸν ἡμιλλημένην
sections.translation: You there ! the one trying with fearful effort to reach the base of the tomb and the pillars of burnt sacrifice , stay where you are . Why do you flee ? I am amazed and speechless at the sight of your body .

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: ἀδικούμεθʼ , ὦ γυναῖκες · εἰργόμεσθα γὰρ
sections.translation: Women , I am being ill-treated . This man is keeping me from the tomb , and he wants to take me and give me to the king , whose wooing I was seeking to avoid .

sections.label: Section 553
sections.original: οὐ κλῶπές ἐσμεν , οὐχ ὑπηρέται κακῶν .
sections.translation: I am no thief , nor a servant of evil men .

sections.label: Section 554
sections.original: καὶ μὴν στολήν γʼ ἄμορφον ἀμφὶ σῶμʼ ἔχεις .
sections.translation: And yet the clothes you are wearing are unsightly enough .

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: στῆσον , φόβου μεθεῖσα , λαιψηρὸν πόδα .
sections.translation: Put fear aside and stop your rapid flight .

sections.label: Section 556
sections.original: ἵστημʼ , ἐπεί γε τοῦδʼ ἐφάπτομαι τόπου .
sections.translation: I do so , now that I have reached this spot .

sections.label: Section 557
sections.original: τίς εἶ ; τίνʼ ὄψιν σήν , γύναι , προσδέρκομαι ;
sections.translation: Who are you ? Whom do I see in you , lady ?

sections.label: Section 558
sections.original: σὺ δʼ εἶ τίς ; αὑτὸς γὰρ σὲ κἄμʼ ἔχει λόγος .
sections.translation: But who are you ? The same reason prompts us both .

sections.label: Section 559
sections.original: οὐπώποτʼ εἶδον προσφερέστερον δέμας .
sections.translation: I never saw a closer resemblance .

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: ὦ θεοί · θεὸς γὰρ καὶ τὸ γιγνώσκειν φίλους .
sections.translation: O gods ! For the recognizing of friends is a god .

sections.label: Section 561
sections.original: Ἑλληνὶς εἶ τις ἢ ἐπιχωρία γυνή ;
sections.translation: Are you a woman from Hellas , or a native of this land ?

sections.label: Section 562
sections.original: Ἑλληνίς · ἀλλὰ καὶ τὸ σὸν θέλω μαθεῖν .
sections.translation: From Hellas ; but I want to learn your story too .

sections.label: Section 564
sections.original: ἐγὼ δὲ Μενέλεῴ γε σέ · οὐδʼ ἔχω τί φῶ .
sections.translation: And you seem to me like Menelaos ; I don ’ t know what to say .

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: ἔγνως γὰρ ὀρθῶς ἄνδρα δυστυχέστατον .
sections.translation: Well , you have correctly recognized a most unfortunate man .

sections.label: Section 566
sections.original: ὦ χρόνιος ἐλθὼν σῆς δάμαρτος ἐς χέρας .
sections.translation: Oh , at last you have come to the arms of your wife !

sections.label: Section 567
sections.original: ποίας δάμαρτος ; μὴ θίγῃς ἐμῶν πέπλων .
sections.translation: What do you mean by wife ? Do not touch my robe .

sections.label: Section 568
sections.original: ἥν σοι δίδωσι Τυνδάρεως , ἐμὸς πατήρ .
sections.translation: The one whom Tyndareus , my father , gave to you .

sections.label: Section 569
sections.original: ὦ φωσφόρʼ Ἑκάτη , πέμπε φάσματʼ εὐμενῆ .
sections.translation: O torch-bearing Hekate , send visions that are favorable !

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: οὐ νυκτίφαντον πρόπολον Ἐνοδίας μʼ ὁρᾷς .
sections.translation: You see in me no specter of the night , attendant on the queen of phantoms .

sections.label: Section 571
sections.original: οὐ μὴν γυναικῶν γʼ εἷς δυοῖν ἔφυν πόσις .
sections.translation: As one man , I am certainly not the husband of two women .

sections.label: Section 572
sections.original: ποίων δὲ λέκτρων δεσπότης ἄλλων ἔφυς ;
sections.translation: You are the master of what other wife ?

sections.label: Section 573
sections.original: ἣν ἄντρα κεύθει κἀκ Φρυγῶν κομίζομαι .
sections.translation: The one hidden in the cave , whom I am bringing from Troy .
